title: microglossa oehleri
intro: Microglossa oehleri, also known as the Oehler's tongue plant, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Microglossa oehleri has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and treat various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for various health benefits such as treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Microglossa oehleri can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
